mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes or produces negative returns in >50% of acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation months or the Federal Reserve explicitly commits to maintaining accommodative policy despite the reading.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth fails to translate into equity gains during stagflation periods, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.3% signals immediate manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive returns in >55% of contraction months or loses significance when global commodity prices are rising.
